title: Using Gold and Silver Vessels
short_rule: It is unlawful to use vessels made of gold and silver for purification, eating, or other purposes.
details: It is not lawful to use gold & silver vessels in purification or otherwise, like eating & makeup. That is because of the statement of the Messenger of Allah: 'Do not drink in gold or silver vessels and do not eat in dishes made of them, for they are for them (unbelievers) in this life and for you in the Hereafter.'
author: Ibn Qudamah al-Maqdisi

title: Entering the Masjid
short_rule: It is recommended to enter the masjid putting the right foot forward and reciting the designated supplication.
details: And when he arrives at the masjid, he should put forward his right foot, and say: 'In the name of Allah, and peace and blessings be upon the Messenger of Allah. O Allah, forgive my sins and open for me the gates of your mercy.'
author: Ibn Qudamah al-Maqdisi

title: Hastening the Iftar
short_rule: It is recommended to hasten to break the fast as soon as the time for the Sunset Prayer has begun.
details: The first recommended action is that one should hasten to break the fast once one is sure that the time for the Sunset Prayer has begun and the time for fasting has ended. It is best to break the fast on a few dates or on water.
author: Abu Shuja' al-Asfahani

title: Passing the Hand over Limbs
short_rule: It is mandatory to actively pass the hand over the washed body parts during ablution and to do the actions in immediate succession.
details: The wajibs of wet ablution are seven and they are: (1) passing (the underside of) one's hand (over the washed part), (2) doing one action of ablution immediately after the other (without a large break)...
remedy_or_consequence: If omitted, the ablution is incomplete and must be repeated or corrected based on the gap time.
author: Ibn 'Ashir

title: Enriching a Single Recipient
short_rule: It is disliked to give a single recipient an amount of Zakat that makes them reach the minimum zakatable wealth threshold.
details: It is disliked to give an amount of wealth to an eligible recipient such that he would then own the nisab [and hence no longer be eligible to receive zakat in the future]. It is preferred, however, to give enough to a recipient such that he need not ask for money for food.
author: Abu 'l-Ikhlas al-Shurunbulali
